paper_title: Neotectonics and Cave Geomorphological Mapping in Niedźwiedzia Cave

methods.description: This method involves recording modifications of original karst morphology by breakdown in caves, distinguishing different grades of breakdown influence on cave passage morphology.

steps.description: Distinguish four grades of breakdown influence on cave passage morphology.
steps.searchable_summary: categorize cave passage morphology based on breakdown influence

steps.description: Estimate breakdown deposit thickness using unmapped passages and vertical distance measurements.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate breakdown deposit thickness using passage and distance data

steps.description: Evaluate the destruction of speleothems, focusing on significant destruction areas.
steps.searchable_summary: assess speleothem destruction in significant areas

methods.description: This method involves collecting speleothem samples from various sites within Niedźwiedzia Cave to study their formation and the cave's geomorphological history.

steps.description: Collect 15 speleothem samples from six sites in Niedźwiedzia Cave.
steps.searchable_summary: collect speleothem samples from specified cave sites

steps.description: Analyze the samples to determine the contact of pre and postevent layers.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze speleothem samples for pre and postevent layer contacts

steps.description: Determine the age of stalagmites and their relation to collapse events.
steps.searchable_summary: determine stalagmite age and relation to collapse events
